p.strong: Backward pass: How skip connections create a gradient highway

div.p: If each ∂fᵢ/∂hᵢ ≈ 0.5, after 12 layers: 0.5¹² ≈ 0.00024 (vanished!) With Residual:
The 1 is the identity term from the skip connection. Even if all ∂fᵢ ≈ 0, gradient ≥ ∂L/∂y (preserved!)
Pre-LN variant shown Each sublayer has a skip connection Gradients flow through both paths
